name: Viennese Beef Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean and thinly slice only the white parts of the leeks.
2. Scald the beef bones and rinse them in cold water.
3. Add the beef and water to a large kettle; bring to a boil.
4. Add the bones, salt, and pepper to the boiling broth. Slowly bring to a full simmer.
5. Remove any scum that rises to the surface of the broth.
6. Cook over low heat, partially covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
7. Again, remove any scum from the top of the soup.
8. Add the remaining ingredients (carrots, celery root, turnips, and cauliflower) and continue cooking about 40 minutes, until meat and vegetables are tender.
9. Remove and discard parsley, bay leaves, and bones.
10. Cut the cooked meat into bite-sized pieces and discard any gristle.
11. Return the meat to the soup.
12. Serves 8 to 10 people. You may also add noodles, rice, or dumplings to the finished soup.
